nvm-sh/nvm.message: Fix `nvm debug` output in `zsh`.

When no `io.js` versions are installed, show `N/A`, not `io.js-`, as the resolved default alias.

Fixes #822.

added_lines: +        NVM_IOJS_VERSION="$(echo "$NVM_IOJS_VERSION" | sed "s/^"$NVM_IMPLICIT"-//" | command grep -e '^v' | command cut -c2- | command cut -d . -f 1,2 | uniq | command tail -1)"

nvm-sh/nvm.message: Consistently prefix builtins with `command`
added_lines: +    LAST_TWO=$(nvm_ls | command grep -e '^v' | cut -c2- | cut -d . -f 1,2 | uniq)
+    LAST_TWO=$(nvm_ls_remote | command grep -e '^v' | cut -c2- | cut -d . -f 1,2 | uniq)

nvm-sh/nvm.message:
Properly handle nvm_print_implicit_alias splitting in zsh.

Hopefully fixes #546.
Make `nvm copy-packages system` work.
removed_lines: -      INSTALLS=$(nvm use "$VERSION" > /dev/null && npm list -g --depth=0 | tail -n +2 | \grep -o -e ' [^@]*' | cut -c 2- | \grep -v npm | xargs)
added_lines: +        INSTALLS=$(nvm deactivate > /dev/null && npm list -g --depth=0 | tail -n +2 | \grep -o -e ' [^@]*' | cut -c 2- | \grep -v npm | xargs)
+        INSTALLS=$(nvm use "$VERSION" > /dev/null && npm list -g --depth=0 | tail -n +2 | \grep -o -e ' [^@]*' | cut -c 2- | \grep -v npm | xargs)

nvm-sh/nvm.message: `npm ls` has a bug in some versions when "--parseable --depth=0" is used together.
removed_lines: -      INSTALLS=$(nvm use $VERSION > /dev/null && npm list --global --parseable --depth=0 2> /dev/null | tail -n +2 | \grep -o -e '/[^/]*$' | cut -c 2- | \grep -v npm | xargs)
added_lines: +      INSTALLS=$(nvm use "$VERSION" > /dev/null && npm list -g --depth=0 | tail -n +2 | \grep -o -e ' [^@]*' | cut -c 2- | \grep -v npm | xargs)

nvm-sh/nvm.message: Use `nvm_normalize_version` in `nvm_version_greater`

Don't try to copy `npm` itself with `nvm copy-packages`.

Fixes #504.
removed_lines: -      INSTALLS=$(nvm use $VERSION > /dev/null && npm list --global --parseable --depth=0 2> /dev/null | tail -n +2 | \grep -o -e '/[^/]*$' | cut -c 2- | xargs)
added_lines: +      INSTALLS=$(nvm use $VERSION > /dev/null && npm list --global --parseable --depth=0 2> /dev/null | tail -n +2 | \grep -o -e '/[^/]*$' | cut -c 2- | \grep -v npm | xargs)

nvm-sh/nvm.message: Use $() instead of backticks; add quotes
Make sure the new `versions` directory is filtered out of nvm_ls output (in zsh).
Should escape grep in copy-packages.
removed_lines: -      INSTALLS=$(nvm use $VERSION > /dev/null && npm list --global --parseable --depth=0 2> /dev/null | tail -n +2 | grep -o -e '/[^/]*$' | cut -c 2- | xargs)
added_lines: +      INSTALLS=$(nvm use $VERSION > /dev/null && npm list --global --parseable --depth=0 2> /dev/null | tail -n +2 | \grep -o -e '/[^/]*$' | cut -c 2- | xargs)

nvm-sh/nvm.message: Should escape grep in nvm_ls
Ensure that `nvm ls node` doesn't return "node_modules", for example.
fix nvm_curl arguments for stdout
Make sure awk is referenced unaliased, per https://github.com/creationix/nvm/issues/162#issuecomment-50284503
Filter out of `nvm ls` things that start with a dot. Fixes #421, closes #422.
add quotes
Redo https://github.com/creationix/nvm/pull/345
removed_lines: -        INSTALLS=( `nvm use $VERSION > /dev/null && npm -g -p ll | \grep "$ROOT\/[^/]\+$" | cut -d '/' -f $(($ROOTDEPTH + 2)) | cut -d ":" -f 2 | \grep -v npm | tr "\n" " "` )
added_lines: +        INSTALLS=`nvm use $VERSION > /dev/null && npm -g -p ll | \grep "$ROOT\/[^/]\+$" | cut -d '/' -f $(($ROOTDEPTH + 2)) | cut -d ":" -f 2 | \grep -v npm | tr "\n" " "`

nvm-sh/nvm.message: Add dist to path, add docs
fix handle cases of grep aliases as described in to resolve #207
removed_lines: -        INSTALLS=`nvm use $VERSION > /dev/null && npm -g -p ll | grep "$ROOT\/[^/]\+$" | cut -d '/' -f 8 | cut -d ":" -f 2 | grep -v npm | tr "\n" " "`
added_lines: +        INSTALLS=`nvm use $VERSION > /dev/null && npm -g -p ll | \grep "$ROOT\/[^/]\+$" | cut -d '/' -f 8 | cut -d ":" -f 2 | \grep -v npm | tr "\n" " "`

nvm-sh/nvm.message: fix copy-packages
removed_lines: -        INSTALLS=`nvm use $VERSION > /dev/null && npm -g -p ll | \grep "$ROOT\/[^/]\+$" | cut -d '/' -f 8 | cut -d ":" -f 2 | \grep -v npm | tr "\n" " "`
added_lines: +        local INSTALLS=`nvm use $VERSION > /dev/null && npm -g -p ll | \grep "$ROOT\/[^/]\+$" | cut -d '/' -f $(($ROOTDEPTH + 2)) | cut -d ":" -f 2 | \grep -v npm | tr "\n" " "`

nvm-sh/nvm.message:
Drop invalid -a flag for basename.

basename doesn't accept options in bash. This causes the VERSIONS variable get the 'N/A' value for $ nvm ls command.

From basename man page:
NAME
       basename - strip directory and suffix from filenames

SYNOPSIS
       basename NAME [SUFFIX]
       basename OPTION

DESCRIPTION
       Print NAME with any leading directory components removed.  If specified, also remove a trailing SUFFIX.

       --help display this help and exit

       --version
              output version information and exit



coreutils version used in Linux Mint 14 Nadia.

GNU coreutils 8.12.197-032bb                                               September 2011                                                               BASENAME(1)

No idea if this flag exists on FreeBSD or other unixes (if they use gnu coreutils shouldn't be any problem)
Make `nvm ls` support listing the "versions" directory when it's present.
make install use a different nvm_version to make not specifying the v avoid clobbering aliases
Fixed incorrect sorting of versions
add support for release directories ( e.g. >= v0.5.1 )
removed_lines: -        for VER in `curl -s http://nodejs.org/dist/ -o - | grep 'node-v.*\.tar\.gz' | sed -e 's/.*node-//' -e 's/\.tar\.gz.*//'`; do
added_lines: +        for VER in `curl -s http://nodejs.org/dist/ -o - | grep 'v[0-9].*' | sed -e 's/.*node-//' -e 's/\.tar\.gz.*//' -e 's/<[^>]*>//' -e 's/\/<[^>]*>.*//'`; do

nvm-sh/nvm.message:
Rewrote version listing system

Now sorts correctly in all instances and prints highlights
even in sorted views. Also will output a list of matching versions
for commands of the form nvm ls 0.4* rather than just printing one
version. nvm version 0.4 still prints the single latest matching
version, however.
Added a fix for issue #59

- version detection now works with zsh
- curl routine now compatible with zsh
- changed curl to use --progress-bar instead of unsafe shorthand (-#)
Use NVM_PATH instead of NODE_PATH
Make the gist work as it's own repo.
added_lines: +      ls "$NVM_DIR" | grep -v src | grep -v nvm.sh | grep -v README.markdown

nvm-sh/nvm.message: fix copy-packages in zsh (without breaking sh and bash)
removed_lines: -        INSTALLS=`nvm use $VERSION > /dev/null && npm -g -p ll | \grep "$ROOT\/[^/]\+$" | cut -d '/' -f 8 | cut -d ":" -f 2 | \grep -v npm | tr "\n" " "`
added_lines: +        INSTALLS=( `nvm use $VERSION > /dev/null && npm -g -p ll | \grep "$ROOT\/[^/]\+$" | cut -d '/' -f 8 | cut -d ":" -f 2 | \grep -v npm | tr "\n" " "` )

nvm-sh/nvm.message: Force unaliased use of ls, for those of us who alias ls in their shell
